title: Lesson 11 — The Workplace

words.definition: wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy
words.example: An indolent afternoon spent dozing in the shade.
The afternoon heat left everyone lethargic.

words.definition: showing a disinclination for exertion; slow and relaxed
words.example: She gave a languid wave from the hammock.
Bored and listless, he stared out the window all day.

words.definition: a state of physical or mental inactivity; sluggishness
words.example: A heavy torpor settled over the office after lunch.

words.definition: slow-moving or slow to respond; inactive
words.example: Sales were sluggish all through the winter.

words.definition: reluctant to work or make an effort; lazy
words.example: A slothful clerk who let the files pile up for weeks.
An industrious student who never missed a deadline.

words.definition: showing careful and persistent effort in work
words.example: Diligent note-taking paid off at exam time.

words.definition: showing dedication, diligence, and steady effort
words.example: Sedulous practice made her fluent within a year.

words.definition: showing great care, attention, and perseverance
words.example: Assiduous editing removed every last error.

words.definition: persisting tirelessly; incapable of being worn out
words.example: An indefatigable volunteer who worked every weekend for a year.

story: The summer heat made the whole office [[lethargic|lethargic]]. Cal was frankly [[indolent|indolent]], [[languid|languid]] at his desk and [[listless|listless]] by noon; a heavy [[torpor|torpor]] settled over him after lunch, and he grew more [[sluggish|sluggish]] and [[slothful|slothful]] by the hour. His deskmate Priya was the opposite: [[industrious|industrious]], [[diligent|diligent]], and [[sedulous|sedulous]] about every detail. Her [[assiduous|assiduous]], almost [[indefatigable|indefatigable]] effort cleared a backlog that had defeated three people before her — and quietly shamed Cal into finally picking up a pen.
